abstract: This paper introduces the Deep Gradient Network (DGNet), a purpose-built framework that leverages object gradient supervision to improve camouflaged object detection (COD). Our approach decouples the detection task into two specialized, interconnected branches: a context encoder for high-level semantics and a texture encoder for structural details. Central to this architecture is the Gradient-Induced Transition (GIT) module, which facilitates a "soft grouping" of features to effectively bridge the gap between contextual and textural information. Despite its streamlined design, DGNet significantly outperforms current state-of-the-art COD models across multiple benchmarks. Specifically, our lightweight variant, DGNet-S, achieves real-time performance (80 fps) and delivers results comparable to the cutting-edge JCSOD model while using only 6.82% of its parameters. Beyond standard camouflage benchmarks, we demonstrate that DGNet generalizes well to diverse downstream tasks, including polyp segmentation, industrial defect detection, and transparent object segmentation. Our code and pre-trained models are available at https://github.com/GewelsJI/DGNet.
